evals.expected_output: A casual trading recap for the current UTC day showing: order activity counts (placed, filled, cancelled), fill rate as a percentage, total volume in USD, a P&L attribution table with separate rows for realized P&L from fills, funding payments, trading fees, and bold net P&L, and a best/worst market callout.
assertions: Response states the number of orders placed, orders filled, and orders cancelled (or confirms zero activity if none occurred)
Response includes a fill rate expressed as a percentage
Response includes total trading volume in USD
Response includes a P&L attribution table (or equivalent structured breakdown) with at minimum: realized P&L, funding payments, trading fees, and net P&L as separate line items
Response identifies the best-performing and worst-performing market by name and net P&L, OR notes that only one market was traded, OR omits this line when no fills occurred
Response does not show unrealized mark-to-market P&L or position values — only realized figures from fills

evals.prompt: show me my realized P&L and fill rate for the last 7 days
evals.expected_output: A detailed recap covering a rolling 7-day window (now minus 7×86400 seconds to now), including: explicit confirmation of the time window in UTC, a per-market breakdown table with realized P&L and fees as separate columns, fill rate percentage for the period, and total volume.
assertions: Response confirms the time window as approximately 7 days ending at the current time, with start and end timestamps shown
Response includes a per-market breakdown table covering all markets traded during the period
Per-market table contains separate columns (or rows) for realized P&L and trading fees — they are not combined into a single figure
Response includes fill rate as a percentage computed from orders placed vs. orders filled
Response includes total volume in USD across the period
Net P&L figure is present and equals realized P&L plus funding minus fees (signs are consistent)

evals.prompt: I think I only closed 2 trades this week — can you compute my win rate?
evals.expected_output: A graceful insufficient-data response for win rate when fewer than 3 closing fills occurred. Response clearly states it cannot compute a meaningful win rate with only 2 closing trades and does not show a win rate percentage, profit factor, or average win/loss figures.
assertions: Response explicitly states that the win rate cannot be computed or is not meaningful due to insufficient closing fills (fewer than 3)
Response does not display a win rate percentage or ratio for the period
Response does not display a profit factor
Response distinguishes closing fills (non-zero realized P&L) from opening fills — if it mentions trade count, it notes that only fills with realized P&L count toward win rate
Response does not show an error message or stack trace — it explains the data limitation in plain language
Response does not fabricate a win rate by using non-closing fills as a proxy or by estimating from the 2 available trades

evals.prompt: recap my trading from 3am to 4am today
evals.expected_output: A graceful empty-state response covering the 03:00–04:00 UTC window today. Response clearly communicates that no fills or orders occurred during the period. No P&L table, no win rate section, and no error message is shown.
assertions: Response confirms the time window queried (03:00–04:00 UTC or equivalent local representation)
Response explicitly states that no fills, no orders, or no trading activity occurred during the period — using plain language such as 'no fills', 'no activity', or 'no trades'
Response does not display a P&L attribution table or any P&L figures when there is no activity
Response does not display a win rate section when there are no closing fills
Response does not show an error, stack trace, or technical failure message
Response does not fabricate placeholder numbers or show zero-filled tables that imply data was retrieved

trigger_evals.query: what did I do last week
am I at risk of liquidation
how many trades did I place
design a trading strategy for ETH
what was my win rate this week

trigger_evals_validation.query: how much slippage did I get on my last trade
what was my fill rate for the last 7 days
show me my P&L breakdown for this month
